root_text.sn38.13:1.6: “Atthi panāvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etesaṁ bhavānaṁ pariññāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.13:1.7: “Atthi kho, āvuso, maggo atthi paṭipadā, etesaṁ bhavānaṁ pariññāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.13:1.8: “Katamo, panāvuso, maggo katamā paṭipadā, etesaṁ bhavānaṁ pariññāyā”ti?
root_text.sn38.13:1.9: “Ayameva kho, āvuso, ariyo aṭṭhaṅgiko maggo, etesaṁ bhavānaṁ pariññāya, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn38.13:1.11: Ayaṁ kho, āvuso, maggo ayaṁ paṭipadā, etesaṁ bhavānaṁ pariññāyā”ti.
root_text.sn38.13:1.12: “Bhaddako, āvuso, maggo bhaddikā paṭipadā, etesaṁ bhavānaṁ pariññāya.

translation_text.sn38.13:0.3: A Question About States of Existence
translation_text.sn38.13:1.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, they speak of this thing called ‘continued existence’.
translation_text.sn38.13:1.3: “Reverend, there are these three states of continued existence.
translation_text.sn38.13:1.4: Existence in the sensual realm, the realm of luminous form, and the formless realm.
translation_text.sn38.13:1.5: These are the three states of continued existence.”
translation_text.sn38.13:1.6: “But, reverend, is there a path and a practice for completely understanding these three states of existence?”
